---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <meta name="description" content="Jesse G — Analytics leader specializing in media measurement, attribution modeling, and reproducible analytics infrastructure.">
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.hero-inner}

[Manager → Director · Analytics Leadership]{.hero-eyebrow}

<!-- # Measurement frameworks that *survive contact* with executives. -->
# Analytics Portfolio.
::: {.hero-tagline}
13+ years building attribution models, automated reporting, anomaly detection systems, and data pipelines infrastructure for media measurement at scale.
:::

::: {.hero-cta-row}
[See the work](#case-studies){.btn-primary-cta}
[Read the methodology paper](posts/code-driven-reporting.qmd){.btn-secondary-cta}
:::

:::
:::


::: {.metrics-row}

::: {.metric}
[13+]{.metric-value}
[Years in analytics]{.metric-label}
[Encore → JuiceMedia]{.metric-detail}
:::


::: {.metric}
[3-7]{.metric-value}
[Analysts managed]{.metric-label}
[Methodology + mentorship]{.metric-detail}
:::

::: {.metric}
[ 150+]{.metric-value}
[Clients supported across campaign measurement, performance, testing and reporting]{.metric-label}
<!-- [Cross-industry campaign experience spanning multiple verticals and business models]{.metric-label} -->
<!-- [Hands-on measurement experience across brand, performance, attribution, and response-driven campaigns]{.metric-detail} -->
:::


::: {.metric}
[Python · R · SQL]{.metric-value style="font-size: 1.4rem;"}
[Stack]{.metric-label}
[AWS  · Tableau · Quarto]{.metric-detail}
:::

:::


[Featured writing]{.section-label}

::: {.featured-essay}
[2025 · Methodology Paper]{.fe-eyebrow}

### [The Case for Code-Driven Reporting: Why Your Analytics Team Should Stop Building Decks](posts/code-driven-reporting.qmd){.stretched-link}

What separates a data team that scales from one that drowns in deliverables. A deep argument for treating reports as products — version-controlled, reproducible, and tested — instead of one-off PowerPoints assembled by hand.

:::


[Case studies]{.section-label}

::: {.section-title #case-studies}
Selected work
:::

::: {.section-intro}
Each case study uses **public or synthetic data.**. Code is on GitHub, methodology is documented, and trade-offs are explained.
:::

::: {.featured-card}

::: {.fc-row}
[Spike Detection · Time-Series Anomaly Detection]{.fc-category}
[Data: Wikipedia Pageviews]{.fc-data}
:::

### [Detecting Media-Driven Spikes in Wikipedia Traffic](case-studies/spike-detection.qmd){.stretched-link}

Built a robust spike detector on three months of real Wikipedia pageview data, then graded it against four planted spikes with known sizes and dates. Caught all four, and surfaced the real news of summer 2024 (the Olympics, the July 13 rally shooting, the DNC) for free.

::: {.fc-tags}
[Python]{.fc-tag} [Polars]{.fc-tag} [Parquet]{.fc-tag} [Time-Series]{.fc-tag} [Robust Statistics]{.fc-tag}
:::

:::

::: {.featured-card}

::: {.fc-row}
[Media Spend Analysis · Real Public Data]{.fc-category}
[Data: FEC Bulk Files]{.fc-data}
:::

### [Political Media Spend Patterns in FEC Operating Expenditures](case-studies/political-ad-spend.qmd){.stretched-link}

Analyzed real political committee disbursements from FEC bulk data — converting messy fixed-width text files to a partitioned Parquet warehouse and surfacing channel-level spend patterns across election cycles.

::: {.fc-tags}
[Python]{.fc-tag} [pyarrow]{.fc-tag} [Parquet Partitioning]{.fc-tag} [DuckDB]{.fc-tag} [R/Plotly]{.fc-tag}
:::

:::

::: {.featured-card}

::: {.fc-row}
[Reporting Engineering · End-to-End Pipeline]{.fc-category}
[Data: CMS Open Payments]{.fc-data}
:::

### [Automated Reporting Pipeline (R + Quarto + gt)](case-studies/automated-pipeline.qmd){.stretched-link}

A reproducible pipeline that ingests real CMS Open Payments data, applies cleaning rules, and renders publication-quality HTML reports and formatted Excel workbooks — with zero manual steps after the first run.

::: {.fc-tags}
[R]{.fc-tag} [Quarto]{.fc-tag} [gt / gtExtras]{.fc-tag} [openxlsx2]{.fc-tag} [arrow]{.fc-tag}
:::

:::


[Engineering POV]{.section-label}

::: {.featured-card style="border-color: var(--accent-bright); background: var(--accent-soft);"}

### [→ Data Foundation: How I Think About the Storage Layer](data-foundation/data-engineering.qmd){.stretched-link}

A walkthrough of the schema, partitioning strategy, and query patterns that underlie the case studies. The boring infrastructure choices that make everything else fast.
:::


[Analysis playbook]{.section-label}

::: {.featured-card}

::: {.fc-row}
[Visualization & Measurement Library]{.fc-category}
[In progress — growing weekly]{.fc-data}
:::

### [Analysis Playbook: Business Questions, Answered Visually](analysis/index.qmd){.stretched-link}

A library of analysis patterns organized by purpose — trends, comparison, distributions, experiment readouts, and KPIs. Each entry pairs a real business question with the right chart, the R code behind it, and the correct statistical read.

::: {.fc-tags}
[R / ggplot2]{.fc-tag} [Trends]{.fc-tag} [Experiment Readouts]{.fc-tag} [Distributions]{.fc-tag} [KPIs]{.fc-tag}
:::

:::


[Toolkit]{.section-label}

::: {.section-title}
What I work with
:::

::: {.skill-grid}
[Python]{.skill .primary}
[R]{.skill .primary}
[SQL]{.skill .primary}
[Polars]{.skill .primary}
[DuckDB]{.skill .primary}
[Parquet / pyarrow]{.skill .primary}
[AWS S3 · Athena]{.skill .primary}
[Quarto]{.skill}
[gt / gtExtras]{.skill}
[Plotly]{.skill}
[Tableau]{.skill}
[Excel · VBA]{.skill}
[Git · GitHub]{.skill}
[Bayesian Methods]{.skill}
[Time-Series Analysis]{.skill}
[Causal Inference]{.skill}
[Anomaly Detection]{.skill}
[A/B & Geo Testing]{.skill}
[Team Leadership]{.skill}
[Executive Communication]{.skill}
:::
